subagent_reports.topic: Target-project routing for adv_worktree_delete/cleanup and actionable triage recommendations

sources.label: target-project.ts withTargetPathStore
sources.summary: Canonical cross-project routing helper. Resolves target project, enforces trust/confirmation gate (mutation && !trusted && !hasConfirmation throws TargetProjectError), ensures target Temporal queue (ensureProjectTemporalQueue), and returns a Store whose paths.root is the TARGET root. stateRequirement 'temporal-required' for mutation, 'snapshot-ok' for read.

sources.label: target-project.ts trust/confirmation gate
sources.summary: Untrusted target_path mutation requires target_confirmed:true + confirmationEvidence before changing target state — exactly the refusal AC2 needs. Reused verbatim, no new auth logic required.

sources.label: contract.ts withContractStore pattern
sources.summary: Reference adopter pattern: local targetArgs object (target_path/target_confirmed/confirmationEvidence) + thin withXStore wrapper delegating to withTargetPathStore with stateRequirement:'temporal-required'. This is the boring template to copy for worktree tools.

sources.label: task.ts cross-project task mutation
sources.summary: rq-crossProjectTaskMutation01 reference: mutation tools route lookup/validation/signal/cache through the target store; target_confirmed/confirmationEvidence in args schema; role 'target' when target_path present.

sources.label: adv-worktree.ts tool definitions
sources.summary: adv_worktree_delete (357), adv_worktree_cleanup (395), adv_worktree_triage (470). All three derive projectRoot=store.paths.root then initWorktreeDb(projectRoot) and pass store into impl deps. triage already takes a projectRoot override arg (482) but its recommendations omit target_path.

sources.label: adv-worktree.ts projectRoot derivation
sources.summary: Every worktree tool computes projectRoot=store.paths.root and database=initWorktreeDb(projectRoot), and threads store into deps. Swapping store for a target store automatically redirects git ops, registry projectId, and Temporal queue — no impl-internal changes needed.

sources.label: worktree/state.ts initStateDb + registry snapshot
sources.summary: initStateDb(projectDir) returns {projectDir, projectId}. getWorktreeRegistrySnapshot keys off access.projectId and queries Temporal workflows `${CHANGE_WORKFLOW_PREFIX}${projectId}/${changeId}` via getService().client. Worktree registry authority is projectId-scoped Temporal change-workflow state, not a sidecar — so redirecting projectRoot+queue is sufficient.

sources.label: worktree/index.ts delete/cleanup deps
sources.summary: AdvWorktreeDeleteDeps and AdvWorktreeCleanupDeps both carry projectRoot+database+log+store. advWorktreeCleanup forwards them to advWorktreeDelete (sole deletion authority). pending_worktree_deletes queue is per-change-workflow Temporal state (projectId-scoped); no separate sidecar to redirect.

sources.label: worktree/triage.ts recommendations
sources.summary: triageWorktrees(repoRoot) is read-only and keys off repoRoot. recommendedFix strings embed bare `adv_worktree_delete <branch>` / `adv_worktree_cleanup` with NO target_path. When triage runs against a target (projectRoot override), these commands are NOT actionable from the current session — they would run against the current project. This is the AC3 gap.

sources.label: worktree-lifecycle spec rq-terminalCleanupSafety01 / rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01
sources.summary: advWorktreeDelete is sole deletion authority; must verify durable ADV terminal state, branch integration, clean tree, no live CWD. Cleanup must stay sequential/bounded (max 5 retries). These MUST gates must remain intact under target routing — routing changes WHICH project's state is checked, not the gates.

sources.label: advance-meta rq-worktreeRegistry01 / rq-multiSessionCoordination01
sources.summary: Worktree state authority lives in change-workflow Temporal state with cross-change visibility via AdvWorktreeBranches/AdvWorktreePaths search attributes; no client-side soft locks (per-process flock only for git worktree add/remove). Cross-session coordination flows through Temporal signals — target routing must keep using the target's Temporal queue, which withTargetPathStore guarantees.

sources.label: adv-review.md cross-project tool-mode law
sources.summary: Established law: target reads via snapshot-ok, target mutations via temporal-required, untrusted mutations include target_confirmed + confirmationEvidence. adv_worktree_delete/cleanup are mutations → temporal-required; adv_worktree_triage is read → snapshot-ok.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Existing pattern: worktree mutation tools (adv_worktree_delete/cleanup) and read tool (adv_worktree_triage) operate on the CURRENT project only. Each tool derives projectRoot=store.paths.root, database=initWorktreeDb(projectRoot) (projectId-scoped), and threads `store` (Temporal queue) into impl deps. Worktree registry/pending-delete authority is projectId-scoped Temporal change-workflow state (rq-worktreeRegistry01), NOT a sidecar DB. Reference pattern: the boring, proven cross-project pattern already used by contract.ts (withContractStore) and task.ts (rq-crossProjectTaskMutation01) — a local targetArgs schema block plus a thin wrapper delegating to withTargetPathStore. withTargetPathStore returns a Store whose paths.root is the target root AND ensures the target Temporal queue, so a single store swap structurally redirects all three worktree dep surfaces (git ops, registry projectId, Temporal queue/pending-delete queue) with no changes inside advWorktreeDelete/Cleanup. Deviation: NONE for the delete/cleanup routing approach (it converges exactly on the canonical helper). The only real gap is AC3: triage recommendedFix strings (triage.ts:178-227) hardcode bare `adv_worktree_delete <branch>` without target_path, so they are non-actionable when triage inspected a different project.

validation.notes: Recommended approach (wrap delete/cleanup in withTargetPathStore with temporal-required; make triage recommendations target-aware) is structurally sound, reuses the established cross-project helper verbatim, and preserves every worktree-lifecycle MUST gate because routing changes only WHICH project's durable state the existing gates evaluate. No spec law requires a new requirement to permit this — it is an additive application of existing cross-project + worktree-authority laws. Risks are scoped and listed in follow_ups, not blocking.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt the contract.ts/task.ts cross-project template — do NOT invent a new routing mechanism. Concretely: (1) Add target_path/target_confirmed/confirmationEvidence to adv_worktree_delete and adv_worktree_cleanup arg schemas (reuse target-project.ts targetPathSchema fields); regenerate public JSON schemas (pnpm run schemas:generate). (2) In each mutation tool's execute, when target_path is present, wrap the body in withTargetPathStore({ currentProjectPath: store.paths.root, target_path, target_confirmed, confirmationEvidence, stateRequirement:'temporal-required' }) and derive projectRoot/database/store from the TARGET scope store; keep the no-target_path path unchanged. The untrusted-mutation refusal (target_path:140-144) satisfies AC2 with zero new auth code. (3) Leave advWorktreeDelete/advWorktreeCleanup impl bodies untouched — they already key entirely off deps.projectRoot/database/store, so all safety gates (terminal/merged/clean/in-use, bounded sequential drain) automatically evaluate the target project. (4) For AC3, make triageWorktrees emit target-aware recommendedFix when called with a non-current repoRoot: thread a target_path/projectRoot hint into the recommendation strings so they read `adv_worktree_delete --target_path <root> <branch>` (and cleanup likewise) and are callable from the current session. Keep triage read-only (snapshot-ok semantics; no mutation) per the Avoidance. (5) Tests: target-path arg acceptance, untrusted-no-confirmation rejection before any deletion, target registry/missing-from-disk repair via target store, dirty/in-use refusal under target routing, and triage recommendation string includes target_path when repoRoot != current. Route suites through bin/oc-test.
follow_ups: Decide the exact target-aware command surface for triage recommendations (e.g. `--target_path <root>` flag text vs a structured field) — confirm with the planner so AC3 'actionable from a different current repo' is met without over-coupling triage to delete CLI string format.

Confirm dryRun semantics for target_path delete/cleanup: per ADV_INSTRUCTIONS dry-run + cross-project, target_path dry-runs may read target state without untrusted-mutation confirmation; ensure the wrapper passes a read path (snapshot-ok or non-mutating) for dryRun:true so preview does not trip the target_confirmed gate.

Verify worktree mutation guard interaction (rq-worktreeMutationGuard01): adv_worktree_delete/cleanup are not gate/task-state transitions, but confirm the guard does not block target-routed cleanup from the current main checkout; the existing tools do not invoke the guard, so likely no change needed — flag for the engineer to confirm during apply.

collectWorktreeCleanupTargets already models a 'target' role and target_worktree_path (cleanup-targets.ts:30-40); check whether archive-flow target cleanup should converge on the same new target_path delete routing to avoid two cross-project cleanup mechanisms.

wisdom_candidates.content: Target-project worktree cleanup routing is cleanest when deletion authority remains in advWorktreeDelete/advWorktreeCleanup and target selection is only a store/context wrapper at the tool boundary.

tests_run: Path preflight: test -e for all scoped files

Review inspection: .adv/specs/worktree-lifecycle/spec.json, docs/specs/worktree-lifecycle.md, plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.ts, plugin/src/tools/worktree/triage.ts, plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.test.ts, plugin/src/tools/worktree/triage.test.ts
verification.evidence: Scoped paths present. Target cleanup requirement rq-worktreeTargetCleanup01 present in spec/docs. adv_worktree_delete and adv_worktree_cleanup route target_path through withTargetPathStore with temporal-required state, mutation gated by !dryRun, target project store/root, and existing advWorktreeDelete/advWorktreeCleanup safety authority preserved. adv_worktree_triage passes currentProjectRoot so target recommendations include target_path/confirmation context. Targeted tests passed: 2 files, 36 tests. git status --short returned clean.

risks: Live ADV tool behavior still requires rebuild/deploy/restart for source changes to affect cached OpenCode plugin runtime; review verified source and tests only.
Direct cleanup unconfirmed-target rejection is covered by shared withTargetPathStore code path; explicit test coverage is strongest for delete routing/rejection and cleanup approved routing.
required_main_agent_actions: Use existing full-suite evidence plus this targeted reviewer verification as acceptance evidence.
Do not revisit out-of-scope worktree lifecycle areas beyond the six scoped files for this acceptance review unless a new finding appears.

consumer_warnings.message: First task-scoped report submission using TASK anchor acceptance-review was rejected because it is not an existing ADV task id; resubmitted as independent change-scoped acceptance review per tool guidance.

wisdom.content: When a read-only ADV triage tool accepts a target project/root and recommends a mutation tool, the mutation tool must support the same target context or the recommendation becomes non-actionable from cross-project sessions. Capture this as spec law, not only operator guidance.

Passing the current store root into read-only triage let recommendation formatting distinguish same-project vs target-project inspections without making triage mutate state or introducing heuristic path guessing.
`bin/oc-test smoke` catches Prettier drift after targeted tests pass; run formatter on touched TypeScript before smoke/full to avoid false implementation regressions.

documents.proposal:
# Add target cleanup

## Intent
Make worktree cleanup tools repair target-project registry/worktrees from a different current ADV session, matching `adv_worktree_triage projectRoot` visibility.

## LBP Targets
- Add explicit target-project routing to cleanup mutation tools without weakening safety gates.
- Preserve dirty/in-use/merged/terminal deletion checks.
- Require explicit confirmation for cross-project mutation.
- Ensure triage recommendations name commands that are callable from the current context.

## Scope
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree*.ts`
- Target-store/cross-project routing helpers as needed
- Tool arg schemas and public schema artifacts if affected
- Worktree cleanup specs/tests/docs

## Success Criteria
- Target-path cleanup can delete/repair stale target-project registry/worktrees after explicit approval.
- Read-only triage recommendations point to callable target-aware cleanup actions.
- Tests cover target-project registry repair and safety refusal.

## Source
Agenda `ag-6vG9FdxN`.
documents.problemStatement: ADV worktree triage can inspect another project via `projectRoot`, but worktree cleanup mutation tools cannot operate on that same target project from the current session, leaving stale target-project registry entries after safe git cleanup.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives
- Add target-project support for worktree cleanup mutations.
- Keep cross-project mutation approval explicit and auditable.
- Preserve all existing deletion safety checks.
- Align triage recommendations with callable cleanup commands.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given an explicit approved `target_path`, when `adv_worktree_delete` is invoked for a target-project stale/missing worktree, then it uses the target project's worktree registry/state and completes or refuses using existing safety gates.
- Given target-project cleanup is requested without explicit confirmation, then mutation is rejected before deletion/registry changes.
- Given `adv_worktree_triage projectRoot` reports target-project drift, then recommended remediation is actionable from a different current repo.
- Given dirty or in-use target worktrees, then cleanup refuses unless existing safe force rules permit it.
- Targeted tests and repo smoke checks pass.

## Constraints
- No direct ADV state file reads/writes.
- No weakening dirty/in-use/merged/terminal safety checks.
- No unapproved cross-project mutation.

## Avoidances
- Do not add shell-workaround-only cleanup paths.
- Do not make `adv_worktree_triage` mutate state.
documents.design:
# Design

## Approach
Add target-project routing to worktree cleanup mutation tools using the existing `withTargetPathStore` pattern.

- `adv_worktree_delete`: add `target_path`, `target_confirmed`, and `confirmationEvidence` args; when `target_path` is present, run existing delete body against the target store with `stateRequirement: "temporal-required"`.
- `adv_worktree_cleanup`: same target-path routing; retain current timeout and dry-run behavior.
- `adv_worktree_triage`: keep read-only, but when `projectRoot` differs from current store root, include target-aware remediation text so output is callable from the current project context.
- Keep `advWorktreeDelete` / `advWorktreeCleanup` safety logic unchanged; target store selection changes the project root, registry, Temporal queue, and worktree DB context structurally.

## LBP Validation
Validated by local architecture research:

- `withTargetPathStore` already gates untrusted cross-project mutation via `target_confirmed:true` and `confirmationEvidence`.
- Worktree wrappers already derive `projectRoot`, `database`, and `store` from the provided store.
- Registry authority is project-scoped Temporal state; target store routing is the correct redirect mechanism.

## Spec-Law Impact
Modify: `worktree-lifecycle`.

Draft spec-delta obligation:

- Capability/spec: `worktree-lifecycle`
- Delta kind: add
- Requirement ID: `rq-worktreeTargetCleanup01`
- Requirement: Worktree deletion and cleanup mutation tools that support target-project operation MUST route target-path calls through the target project store/Temporal queue, require explicit confirmation for untrusted target mutation, and preserve existing delete/cleanup safety gates. Worktree triage target-project recommendations MUST be actionable from the current session by including target-aware remediation.
- Rationale: `adv_worktree_triage projectRoot` can inspect target-project drift, but cleanup mutation tools previously lacked matching target-project operation, leaving registry drift unrepaired from cross-project sessions.
- Scenario 1: Given `adv_worktree_delete` is called with an untrusted `target_path` and no confirmation, when the tool validates args, then it refuses before filesystem or registry mutation.
- Scenario 2: Given `adv_worktree_delete` is called with an approved `target_path`, when it evaluates deletion, then it uses the target project's root, registry, and Temporal queue while preserving dirty/in-use/merged/terminal safety gates.
- Scenario 3: Given `adv_worktree_triage projectRoot` inspects a different project, when it reports a cleanup recommendation, then the recommendation includes target-aware remediation usable from the current session.

## Validation Plan
- Unit tests for target-path routing on delete and cleanup.
- Unit test for unconfirmed target mutation refusal.
- Unit tests for target-aware triage recommendation text.
- Existing worktree tests for delete/cleanup safety remain green.
- Smoke verification via `bin/oc-test smoke`.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

Implemented target-project worktree cleanup support for ADV.

## Outcome
- `adv_worktree_delete` and `adv_worktree_cleanup` now accept target-project arguments and route approved target operations through the target project's store and Temporal queue.
- Untrusted target mutations require explicit `target_confirmed: true` and `confirmationEvidence`; dry-run target reads remain non-mutating.
- `adv_worktree_triage` now formats cross-project remediation with target-aware cleanup arguments instead of bare current-project commands.
- Added `rq-worktreeTargetCleanup01` to `worktree-lifecycle` spec and docs.

## Verification
- `pnpm run schemas:check` passed.
- `pnpm test -- src/tools/adv-worktree.test.ts src/tools/worktree/triage.test.ts` passed.
- `bin/oc-test smoke` passed.
- `bin/oc-test full` passed.
- Independent reviewer verdict: READY, no blocking findings.
documents.acceptance:
# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-03T19:02:41.949Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | Given an explicit approved `target_path`, when `adv_worktree_delete` is invoked for a target-project stale/missing worktree, then it uses the target project's worktree registry/state and completes or refuses using existing safety gates. | pass | `plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.test.ts` target_path delete test passed via `pnpm test -- src/tools/adv-worktree.test.ts src/tools/worktree/triage.test.ts` and `bin/oc-test full`. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | Given target-project cleanup is requested without explicit confirmation, then mutation is rejected before deletion/registry changes. | pass | `adv_worktree_delete rejects unconfirmed target mutation before deleting` test passed; full suite passed. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | Given `adv_worktree_triage projectRoot` reports target-project drift, then recommended remediation is actionable from a different current repo. | pass | `plugin/src/tools/worktree/triage.test.ts` target_path remediation test passed; full suite passed. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | Given dirty or in-use target worktrees, then cleanup refuses unless existing safe force rules permit it. | pass | Code review confirmed existing `advWorktreeDelete`/`advWorktreeCleanup` bodies remain safety authorities; target routing only changes store/root context. adv-reviewer verdict READY. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | Targeted tests and repo smoke checks pass. | pass | `pnpm run schemas:check` passed; targeted tests passed; `bin/oc-test smoke` passed; `bin/oc-test full` passed. |
| C1 | constraint | No direct ADV state file reads/writes. | respected | No direct ADV state file reads/writes used; implementation routes through target store and worktree state helpers. |
| C2 | constraint | No weakening dirty/in-use/merged/terminal safety checks. | respected | Existing deletion and cleanup functions remain sole authorities; no safety checks removed. adv-reviewer READY. |
| C3 | constraint | No unapproved cross-project mutation. | respected | Unconfirmed target mutation rejection test passed; target routing uses `withTargetPathStore` confirmation gate. |
| DONT1 | avoidance | Do not add shell-workaround-only cleanup paths. | respected | No shell-workaround cleanup path added; solution uses tool/store architecture. |
| DONT2 | avoidance | Do not make `adv_worktree_triage` mutate state. | respected | Triage remains read-only and only formats recommendations. |

clarify_findings.message: Proposal describes behavior that could fail but doesn't mention error handling or rollback. What happens when things go wrong?
